brief_justification: Arrives at the exact correct answer (No) with accurate arithmetic (9,940C vs 6,983C); identifies the core tension between marginal purity gain and volume throughput loss, explaining second-order effect of diminishing returns at 99.4% baseline; no explicit bias-naming or assumption-checking, just linear computation without reflective pauses; verifies by computing both scenarios and providing an intuitive explanation but no counterfactual or alternate derivation angle; uses C as a variable implicitly acknowledging concentration is a parameter but never explicitly states assumptions or confidence bounds; only evaluates the two given scenarios with no exploration of break-even flow rate or other alternatives; every derivation step is shown in tables with traceable fractions, fully reproducible by a third party.
